commands.review_summary:
The PR makes `memory_search` skip the forced zero-hit sync/retry only when the active memory manager reports the `qmd` backend, and adds a regression test for that QMD path.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +23. Total +27 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `memory_search` awaits forced sync after zero hits, and QMD sync awaits update work. I did not run a live QMD timing reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real QMD proof or maintainer acceptance of the compatibility tradeoff, not a narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to generate.

target.title: fix(memory-core): skip qmd zero-hit search sync
target.body:
## What changed

Fixes #90023.

`memory_search` no longer runs the synchronous zero-hit forced sync path when the active memory backend is `qmd`. Non-QMD backends keep the existing zero-hit repair behavior.

## Behavior proof

Setup:

```text
Repository: openclaw/openclaw
Base: origin/main at 2b31ad2e
Worktree: /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-90023
Implementation path: Fastino Code TUI, DeepSeek V4 Flash Free, XML+SS, SLM lane, max
```

Before:

```ts
if (rawResults.length === 0 && activeMemory.manager.sync) {
  await activeMemory.manager.sync({ reason: "search", force: true });
  rawResults = await activeMemory.manager.search(query, searchOptions);
}
```

For a QMD-backed zero-hit search, that condition allowed a synchronous forced `manager.sync({ reason: "search", force: true })` inside the interactive `memory_search` path.

After:

```ts
if (
  rawResults.length === 0 &&
  activeMemory.manager.sync &&
  statusBeforeRetry.backend !== "qmd"
) {
  await activeMemory.manager.sync({ reason: "search", force: true });
  rawResults = await activeMemory.manager.search(query, searchOptions);
}
```

The new regression test sets the memory backend to `qmd`, returns zero hits, and asserts:

```text
results == []
searchCalls == 1
getMemorySyncMockCalls() == 0
```

## How to test

```sh
pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Results:

```text
pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       16 passed (16)

pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       34 passed (34)

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check
No output.
```

## Platforms tested

- macOS local worktree
- Node/Vitest through the repository `pnpm test` wrapper

## Limitations

This is source-level and unit-level verification. I did not run a live QMD binary timing test, so the PR proves the synchronous forced sync is skipped for the QMD zero-hit tool path rather than measuring a live `qmd update` delay.
target.author: sahibzada-allahyar
